verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (HSP60) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin, but it is one of the best-documented examples of a stress/cancer-induced ecto-chaperone. Under heat shock, oxidative stress, and especially in tumor cells, HSP60 translocates to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane where it is detectable by surface biotinylation, flow cytometry, and antibody binding on intact cells. This cancer-cell ecto-pool has been exploited therapeutically (anti-HSP60 antibodies targeting tumor cell surfaces). It is also externalized during immunogenic cell death and apoptosis, qualifying under cell_state_induced. No TM domain exists; the surface display is strictly cell-state-dependent. The NCBI note on 'signaling molecule in the innate immune system' is consistent with this extracellular / surface-associated biology.
